full_prompt: Design the primary structural shell and internal stack frame of a CanSat complying with the national aerospace student benchmark 2025 Model Uydu Yarışması şartnamesi. The complete CanSat including all payloads, batteries, electronics, and recovery hardware must fit within a cylindrical envelope of outer diameter 66 mm (-0/+1 mm) and total height 115 mm (-0/+2 mm), with total integrated wet mass between 300 g and 350 g (inclusive of parachute assembly). The structure comprises a thin-wall cylindrical outer skin, a conical nose cap, a base plate with parachute eyelet, and three internal PCB-stack rails spaced 120 degrees around the inner wall. The CanSat is launched inside a carrier rocket to an altitude of approximately 700 m AGL, ejected, and descends under a single parachute with a nominal descent rate between 6 m/s and 8 m/s measured over the last 100 m before touchdown. Material is 3D-printed PA12-CF (carbon-fiber reinforced nylon, SLS) with Young's modulus 5.8 GPa, tensile strength 75 MPa, density 1060 kg/m^3, and isotropic assumption acceptable at shell level. Load cases: (LC1) 20 g quasi-static axial acceleration applied to the full CanSat wet mass of 350 g (corresponds to the qualification-level launch shock specified in the competition safety review); (LC2) 15 g lateral acceleration applied simultaneously to the full wet mass along the worst-case transverse direction; (LC3) parachute opening shock of 40 N applied at the base-plate eyelet in the axial direction while the CanSat hangs from the shock cord; (LC4) ground-impact drop of 350 g at 8 m/s onto a rigid flat surface (use an equivalent static 10 g deceleration on the base for first-order sizing). Pass/fail criteria: (R1) maximum von Mises stress in the primary structure must remain below 37.5 MPa under all load cases (75 / 2.0 material factor of safety); (R2) maximum deflection of any PCB stack rail relative to the outer skin must remain below 1.5 mm under LC1 to avoid PCB-to-wall contact; (R3) no structural-element first natural frequency below 80 Hz to avoid coupling with the carrier rocket motor thrust oscillation band; (R4) total structural dry mass (shell + nose + base + rails, excluding PCB/battery/parachute) must not exceed 120 g to leave mass budget for payload; (R5) the shell outer surface must not protrude past the 66 mm envelope under any load case. Vibration qualification is satisfied by meeting R3 plus the LC1/LC2 quasi-static envelope; no random-vibration PSD is imposed by the rulebook at this level. Thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 solid model, SLS process note (build orientation and support plan), FEA report with stress + deflection for each load case, and modal report listing the first 10 eigenfrequencies.
short_prompt:
Design the primary structural shell and internal stack frame of a national aerospace student benchmark 2025 Model Uydu CanSat. Cylindrical envelope 66 mm (-0/+1) OD x 115 mm (-0/+2) height; total integrated wet mass 300-350 g. Launched in a carrier rocket to ~700 m AGL and descends at 6-8 m/s under one parachute.

Envelope: thin-wall cylindrical outer skin, conical nose, base plate with parachute eyelet, and three internal PCB-stack rails at 120 deg around the wall; 3D-printed PA12-CF (SLS), isotropic at shell level.

Factor regime: material FoS 2.0 (limit 37.5 MPa); deflection / envelope / modal gates; no random-vib PSD mandated at this level.

Load cases.
- 20 g axial quasi-static launch on 350 g wet.
- 15 g lateral quasi-static on 350 g (worst-transverse).
- Parachute-opening shock: 40 N axial at the base-plate eyelet.
- Ground impact: 8 m/s onto rigid flat, first-order equivalent 10 g static deceleration on the base.
- First natural frequency >= 80 Hz.

prompt.title: national aerospace student benchmark 2025 Model Uydu Primary CanSat Structure
prompt.brief: 350 g CanSat primary shell + stack frame, 66 x 115 mm envelope, 20 g axial + 15 g lateral launch qual, parachute descent 6-8 m/s.

load_cases.name: axial_launch_20g
load_cases.application: axial on full 350 g wet mass

load_cases.name: lateral_launch_15g
load_cases.application: transverse on full 350 g wet mass

load_cases.name: ground_impact
load_cases.application: base equivalent static for 8 m/s drop

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: TET10 solid for shell and rails

exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Short mission (< 20 min), no thermal gradient imposed by rulebook at this class.
exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Descent drag reduced to parachute opening shock LC3.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Thin-wall check covered by LC1 axial stress gate.
